areaTag.zh: 家事法 · 程序 · Harman 承诺

one.zh: 程序问题——丈夫申请解除 Harman 默示承诺，以便将家事诉讼中的宣誓证据用于维多利亚州最高法院的诉讼；法院认定未构成「特殊情形」，驳回申请。
one.en: Practice & procedure — the husband sought release from the Harman undertaking to use family-law affidavit evidence in Supreme Court of Victoria proceedings; the court found no 'special circumstances' and dismissed the application.

issue.zh: 丈夫能否证明存在「特殊情形」，从而解除他对本院的默示（Harman）承诺与 s 114Q、规则 6.04 的限制，以便把本案两份宣誓书用于最高法院的移交申请？
issue.en: Has the husband shown 'special circumstances' to be released from the implied (Harman) undertaking and the restrictions in s 114Q and r 6.04, so as to use two affidavits filed here in his Supreme Court transfer application?

summary.catchwords: FAMILY LAW — Practice and procedure – Application for release from Harman undertaking – Where the husband seeks leave to use affidavit evidence filed in proceedings under the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) for the purpose of proceedings in the Supreme Court of Victoria – Where the affidavits sought to be used traverse personal and sensitive information of the parties and their adult children – Consideration of authorities on special circumstances – Special circumstances not established – Insufficient nexus between the proceedings – Leave refused

subseq.zh: 本案为终局裁定（驳回申请）；费用问题留待书面裁定。本资料库未见进一步记录。
subseq.en: This is a final ruling (application dismissed); costs reserved for determination on the papers. No further record in this library.

hint.en: The whole case as a story

html.zh:
双方正在本院进行财产分割诉讼：妻 Ms Florou 于 2025-12-19 起诉，请求依《家庭法》s 79 调整财产、并就成年子女抚养及一系列临时命令（含配偶赡养）提出请求；夫 Mr Florou 于 2026-02-09 答辩，亦请求调整财产。本院诉讼尚处早期，若需开庭最早 2027 年。

与此同时，妻在维多利亚州最高法院另案起诉夫：指控夫在一份提交给第三方的法定声明（Statutory Declaration）中诽谤她、违反保密义务、侵犯其隐私，索赔（含惩罚性赔偿）。背景是：2025 年初，夫的社交账号上出现一张其露骨照片，夫否认系本人发布；在随后两项调查中，夫发表法定声明，妻称其中披露了她的性史、心理健康、病史、私人关系及与执法机关接触等信息，并被广泛传播。最高法院案预计 2026 年底开庭、审期 5–7 天。

夫在最高法院案中申请依《跨法院授权法》把该案移交本院（Transfer Application），主张两案构成「同一个可裁判的争议」。为支持移交申请，夫于 2026-04-10 提出本程序内申请：请求解除他对本院的默示承诺（Harman 承诺），以及 s 114Q、规则 6.04 施加的保密义务，以便使用本案两份宣誓书——夫 2026-02-09 的宣誓书、妻 2025-12-19 的宣誓书。

妻反对，并请求夫承担费用。CasePocket：于是法院要回答一个程序问题——夫是否证明存在「特殊情形」，足以解除 Harman 承诺。Johns 法官的答案是否定的，申请被驳回。
html.en:
The parties are running property-settlement proceedings here: the wife, Ms Florou, applied on 19 Dec 2025 for alteration of property under s 79 of the Family Law Act, plus adult-child maintenance and interim orders (including spousal maintenance); the husband, Mr Florou, responded on 9 Feb 2026 also seeking a property adjustment. These proceedings are at an early stage; any trial would not be before 2027.

Meanwhile the wife sued the husband separately in the Supreme Court of Victoria, alleging that in a Statutory Declaration given to third parties he defamed her, breached her confidence and invaded her privacy, claiming damages (including exemplary damages). The background: in early 2025 a sexually explicit image of the husband appeared on his social-media account; he denied posting it, and in two ensuing investigations published a Statutory Declaration which the wife says disclosed her sexual history, mental health, medical history, personal relationships and engagement with law enforcement, and was widely disseminated. The Supreme Court matter is listed for a 5–7 day hearing in late 2026.

In the Supreme Court matter the husband issued a summons to transfer it to this Court under the cross-vesting legislation (the Transfer Application), arguing the two cases are a single justiciable controversy. To support that, on 10 Apr 2026 he applied here to be released from his implied (Harman) undertaking and from the confidentiality duties in s 114Q and r 6.04, so he could use two affidavits filed here — his of 9 Feb 2026 and the wife's of 19 Dec 2025.

The wife opposed and sought costs. CasePocket: so the court faced one procedural question — had the husband shown 'special circumstances' to be released from the Harman undertaking? Johns J said no, and dismissed the application.

hint.en: The judge's path, step by step

t.zh: 判决路径 · 案件走向

html.zh:
1
起点：当事人对法院负有默示承诺（Harman）——诉讼中被强制披露/提交的文件，未经法院许可只能用于本案（规则 6.04 将其成文化）。
2
例外：规则 r 15.26 ——须证明存在「特殊情形」方可许可他用；举证责任在夫。 ›
3
「特殊情形」的判断（Springfield Nominees）：需有使本案不同寻常的特别之处；裁量宽泛，最重要因素是该文件对另案实现公正的可能贡献。 ›
4
可考虑文件在另案的目的/用途，但不判断其可采性（Kuang、Littlefield）。 ›
5
两案是否有关联/共同争议？本院＝s 79 财产（认定与估值、贡献、现况与将来）；最高法院＝就一份法定声明的诽谤/保密/隐私。法院认定无关联——「除当事人相同外，几无共同之处」；且妻让步：不以家暴主张财产调整。
6
即使存在特殊情形，裁量也不应解除：宣誓书含当事人及成年子女的高度敏感信息、为本案而作并受 s 114Q 保护、公开法庭有外泄风险、夫几乎未举证说明如何受损。 ›
7
结论：未证明特殊情形 → 申请驳回。费用问题留待书面裁定。
html.en:
1
Start: parties owe the court an implied (Harman) undertaking — documents compulsorily disclosed/filed in litigation may not be used outside the proceeding without the court's leave (codified in r 6.04).
2
Exception: r 15.26 — the court may permit other use only if 'special circumstances' are shown; the onus is on the husband. ›
3
Special circumstances (Springfield Nominees): a special feature taking the case beyond the ordinary; a wide discretion, the most important factor being the document's likely contribution to justice in the second proceeding. ›
4
The court may consider the purpose/use in the other court, but not its admissibility (Kuang; Littlefield). ›
5
Is there a nexus / commonality? Here = s 79 property (identification/valuation, contributions, present & future); Supreme Court = defamation/confidence/privacy over one Statutory Declaration. The court found no nexus — "little commonality… other than the parties being the same"; and the wife conceded she would not seek a property adjustment for family violence.
6
Even if special circumstances existed, the discretion would not be exercised: the affidavits hold highly sensitive information about the parties and their adult children, were sworn for these proceedings and protected by s 114Q, carry open-court leakage risk, and the husband led scant evidence of disadvantage. ›
7
Conclusion: special circumstances not established → application dismissed. Costs reserved for the papers.

html.zh:

CasePocket：s 79、s 114Q 出自《家庭法 1975》；r 6.04、r 15.26 出自《联邦巡回与家事法院（家事法）规则 2021》。本案核心是后两条——默示承诺的成文化与「特殊情形」例外。
html.en:
§ s 79 ›
§ s 114Q ›
§ r 6.04 ›
§ r 15.26 ›
CasePocket: s 79 and s 114Q are in the Family Law Act 1975; r 6.04 and r 15.26 are in the FCFCOA (Family Law) Rules 2021. The case turns on the last two — the codified undertaking and the 'special circumstances' exception.

hint.en: Why the judge decided this way

html.zh:
1. 可以审视另案的目的与用途。Johns 法官据 Kuang 认为，判断是否解除 Harman 承诺时，法院可以考虑文件在另案的目的或可能用途——否则无从评估「该文件对另案实现公正的可能贡献」等因素。但这不等于由本院去判断该文件在另案是否可采。

2. 两案没有共同争议。本院的财产之诉适用 s 79：需识别与估值财产、评估双方在关系中的贡献、考量现况与将来。最高法院之诉则是另一回事：夫向第三方散布其法定声明，是否构成《诽谤法 2005（维）》下的诽谤、违反保密或侵犯隐私。原文："Other than the parties in both proceedings being the same, there is little commonality in the issues confronting each Court." 妻还作出让步——不会因家暴主张财产调整，故其健康至多与「贡献或将来情形」边缘相关。

3. 即便存在特殊情形，裁量也不应解除。法院尤其顾及四点：① 宣誓书含当事人及成年子女（其中一名有遗传病与智力障碍、受 NDIS 资助）的高度敏感信息；② 宣誓书为本案而作、受 s 114Q 保密保护；③ 两案除当事人相同外几无共同之处；④ 夫几乎未举证说明若不能使用这些文件他将如何受损、文件与最高法院诉讼有何相关。夫虽称已在最高法院申请文件保密，但「他申请不等于法院会准」，外泄风险仍在。

4. 结论。原文："I am not satisfied that the husband has established that a special circumstance exists to permit his release from the Harman obligation… even if such special circumstance were established, I am not satisfied that the discretion… ought be exercised." 申请驳回。
html.en:
1. The court may look at purpose and use in the other case. Following Kuang, Johns J held the court may consider the documents' purpose or possible use in the other proceeding — otherwise it cannot weigh factors such as their "likely contribution to achieving justice" there. But that is not the same as this court deciding whether the documents would be admissible in the other court.

2. No commonality of issues. The property case here applies s 79: identifying and valuing property, assessing contributions, and considering present and future circumstances. The Supreme Court case is a different inquiry: whether the husband's dissemination of his Statutory Declaration defamed the wife under the Defamation Act 2005 (Vic), breached her confidence or invaded her privacy. "Other than the parties in both proceedings being the same, there is little commonality in the issues confronting each Court." The wife also conceded she would not seek a property adjustment for family violence, so her health is at most marginally relevant to contributions or future needs.

3. Even if special circumstances existed, the discretion would not be exercised. The court weighed four matters: (i) the affidavits hold highly sensitive information about the parties and their adult children (one with a genetic condition and intellectual disability, supported under the NDIS); (ii) they were sworn for these proceedings and protected by s 114Q; (iii) beyond identical parties there is little commonality; and (iv) the husband led scant evidence of how he would be disadvantaged or how the documents were relevant to the Supreme Court case. That he had sought confidentiality orders there did not ensure they would be made; the leakage risk remained.

4. Conclusion. "I am not satisfied that the husband has established that a special circumstance exists to permit his release from the Harman obligation… even if such special circumstance were established, I am not satisfied that the discretion… ought be exercised." The application was dismissed.

hint.en: What you can cite it for

t.zh: 本案确立什么 · 可援引于
t.en: What this case establishes · cite for

html.zh:
确立/重申：

① 解除家事诉讼中的默示（Harman）承诺须经 r 15.26「特殊情形」之门，举证责任在申请人；判断时可考虑文件在另案的目的/用途，但不判断可采性（重申 Kuang、Littlefield）。

② 仅当事人相同不构成「共同争议」——须看两案争议的实质（此处 s 79 财产 vs 诽谤/隐私）。

③ 即使满足特殊情形，仍是裁量问题；涉及当事人及子女的高度敏感信息、文件为诉讼而作并受 s 114Q 保护、公开法庭外泄风险，都是不予解除的重要理由。

可援引于：反对将家事诉讼中的宣誓证据「搬到」其他法院/法域使用；强调对敏感个人及子女信息的保护；提醒申请人须切实举证文件与另案的相关性与对公正的贡献。
html.en:
Establishes / reaffirms:

(1) Release from the implied (Harman) undertaking in family-law litigation runs through the r 15.26 'special circumstances' gate, with the onus on the applicant; the court may weigh the documents' purpose/use in the other proceeding but not their admissibility (reaffirming Kuang and Littlefield).

(2) Identity of parties alone is not 'commonality' — look to the substance of the issues in each case (here s 79 property vs defamation/privacy).

(3) Even where special circumstances exist, release remains discretionary; sensitive information about the parties and their children, documents created for the litigation and protected by s 114Q, and open-court leakage risk are powerful reasons to refuse.

Cite it for: resisting the 'export' of family-law affidavit evidence into other courts/jurisdictions; protecting sensitive personal and children's information; and requiring an applicant to show real relevance and contribution to justice in the other proceeding.

html.zh:
Harman undertaking / implied undertaking默示承诺——诉讼中被强制披露的文件只能用于本案，未经法院许可不得他用
special circumstances特殊情形——解除默示承诺的法定门槛
s 79 (property alteration)财产权益的变动（家事财产分割的核心条文）
s 114Q限制向公众传播可识别当事人的程序报道（保密保护）
cross-vesting / Transfer Application跨法院授权 / 移交申请——请求将另一法院的案件移交本院
Statutory Declaration法定声明（具法律效力的书面声明）
nexus / commonality of issues关联 / 共同争议
determined on the papers书面裁定（不开庭，依书面材料在内庭决定）
html.en:
Harman / implied undertakingDocuments compulsorily disclosed in litigation may be used only for that proceeding, not otherwise without the court's leave
special circumstancesThe statutory threshold for release from the undertaking
s 79 (property alteration)Power to alter property interests — the core financial-settlement provision
s 114QRestriction on publishing accounts of proceedings that identify the parties (confidentiality)
cross-vesting / Transfer ApplicationSeeking to move a case from another court into this Court
Statutory DeclarationA formal written statement made under statute
nexus / commonality of issuesA real connection / shared issues between proceedings
determined on the papersDecided in chambers on written submissions, without a hearing

sc.en: In case: alteration of property interests

desc.zh: 赋予法院在婚姻破裂后调整双方财产权益的权力：识别与估值财产、评估各自在关系中的贡献、并考量双方现况与将来情形。本案的本院诉讼即以 s 79 为依据。
desc.en: Empowers the court to alter the parties' property interests after a marriage breaks down: identifying and valuing property, assessing each party's contributions, and considering present and future circumstances. The proceedings in this Court are brought under s 79.

src.zh: 法院以 s 79 之诉的性质与最高法院诽谤之诉相对照，认定两者争议不同。
src.en: The court contrasted the nature of the s 79 claim with the Supreme Court defamation claim to find the issues differed.

sc.zh: 本案：程序文件的保密保护

desc.zh: 属第 XIVB 编：除极有限情形外，向公众传播可识别家事诉讼当事人（或相关人、证人）的程序报道构成罪行。本案宣誓书为本程序而作，因而享有此项保密保护。
desc.en: In Part XIVB: except in very limited cases, communicating to the public an account of family-law proceedings that identifies the parties (or associated persons or witnesses) is an offence. The affidavits here were filed for these proceedings and so enjoy this confidentiality protection.

src.zh: 法院将 s 114Q 的保护作为不予解除承诺的重要理由之一。
src.en: The court relied on the s 114Q protection as one reason not to release the undertaking.

sc.zh: 本案：文件仅限本程序使用
sc.en: In case: documents for this proceeding only

desc.zh: 将默示承诺成文化：除非法院许可，查阅、复制或收到诉讼文件之人，只能将该文件用于本程序，且不得向他人披露其内容或提供副本。
desc.en: Codifies the implied undertaking: unless the court permits, a person who inspects, copies or receives a document in a proceeding must use it only for that proceeding, and must not disclose its contents or give a copy to anyone else.

src.zh: 夫申请解除的，正是 r 6.04（及 Harman 承诺、s 114Q）所施加的义务。
src.en: It is the obligation under r 6.04 (with the Harman undertaking and s 114Q) that the husband sought to be released from.

sc.zh: 本案：经「特殊情形」可许可他用
sc.en: In case: other use on 'special circumstances'

desc.zh: 法院可许可某人将诉讼中取得的文件或信息用于另一目的（包括其他法院或仲裁庭的程序），前提是该人证明存在「特殊情形」。这是解除默示承诺的法定通道，本案的判断核心。
desc.en: The court may permit a person to use a document or information obtained in proceedings for another purpose (including in another court or tribunal) if that person shows 'special circumstances'. This is the statutory gateway to release, and the focus of this case.

src.zh: 法院认定夫未证明 r 15.26 所要求的「特殊情形」。
src.en: The court held the husband had not shown the 'special circumstances' r 15.26 requires.

t.zh: Harman v Secretary of State for the Home Department
t.en: Harman v Secretary of State for the Home Department

summary.zh: 确立「默示承诺」原则：在诉讼中被强制披露文件的一方，未经法院许可，不得将其用于该诉讼以外的附带目的。
summary.en: Established the 'implied undertaking': a party who obtains documents by compulsory disclosure in litigation may not use them for collateral purposes without the court's leave.

prop.en: The source of the implied (Harman) undertaking.

src.zh: 本案以 Harman 原则为出发点。
src.en: The case takes the Harman principle as its starting point.

note.zh: ✦ 经典英国判例（未纳入本资料库）。
note.en: ✦ Leading UK authority (not in this library).

summary.zh: 高等法院在澳大利亚确认默示承诺：被强制披露文件/信息的一方，除非已被采纳为证据，否则未经法院许可不得用于取得目的以外。
summary.en: The High Court affirmed the undertaking in Australia: a party compelled to disclose documents/information cannot use them for any purpose other than that for which they were given, unless received into evidence, without the court's leave.

prop.zh: 默示承诺在澳大利亚的权威确认。
prop.en: Authoritative Australian confirmation of the undertaking.

HearneStreet.passage: "Where one party to litigation is compelled… to disclose documents or information, the party obtaining the disclosure cannot, without the leave of the court, use it for any purpose other than that for which it was given unless it is received into evidence." (at [154])

src.zh: 本案据 Hearne v Street 确认该承诺及 r 6.04 的成文化。
src.en: The case relies on Hearne v Street for the undertaking and its codification in r 6.04.

note.zh: ✦ 高等法院判例（未纳入本资料库）。
note.en: ✦ High Court authority (not in this library).

t.zh: Springfield Nominees Pty Ltd v Bridgelands Securities Ltd
t.en: Springfield Nominees Pty Ltd v Bridgelands Securities Ltd

summary.zh: Wilcox 法官阐述「特殊情形」：只需有使本案不同寻常、构成解除理由的特别之处；其后是裁量，相关因素众多（文件性质、是否为诉讼而作、是否含个人/商业敏感信息，以及最重要的——文件对另案实现公正的可能贡献）。
summary.en: Wilcox J explained 'special circumstances': it is enough that there is a special feature affording a reason to release; thereafter the matter is discretionary, with many factors (the nature of the document, whether created for the litigation, whether it holds personal/commercially sensitive information, and most importantly its likely contribution to justice in the second proceeding).

prop.zh: 「特殊情形」的判准与裁量因素清单。
prop.en: The test for 'special circumstances' and the list of discretionary factors.

Springfield.passage: "For 'special circumstances' to exist it is enough that there is a special feature of the case which affords a reason for modifying or releasing the undertaking… many factors being relevant… perhaps most important of all, the likely contribution of the document to achieving justice in the second proceeding."

src.zh: 本案以 Springfield 的因素框架衡量并认定不予解除。
src.en: The case applied Springfield's framework and refused release.

note.zh: ✦ 领先联邦法院判例（未纳入本资料库）。
note.en: ✦ Leading Federal Court authority (not in this library).

court.zh: FCFCOA 第 1 庭（全席上诉）· 2025

summary.zh: 全席法院总结「特殊情形」判例，并确认：法院可考虑文件在另案的目的或可能用途，但这与判断该文件在另案是否可采，性质不同。
summary.en: The Full Court summarised the 'special circumstances' authorities and confirmed the court may consider the purpose or possible use of the document in the other proceeding — qualitatively different from deciding whether it would be admissible there.

prop.zh: 解除承诺时，可考虑「用途/目的」，但不判断「可采性」。

Kuang.passage: "…the court from which the release is sought can, and will often need to, have regard… to the purpose or possible use of that document in the other court… However, that is qualitatively different from the first court determining whether the document could actually be used, and be admissible in, the other court." (at [49])

src.zh: 本案据 Kuang 审视文件在最高法院诉讼的目的/用途。
src.en: The case followed Kuang in examining the documents' purpose/use in the Supreme Court matter.

note.zh: ✦ 同庭全席上诉判例（未纳入本资料库）。
note.en: ✦ Full Court authority (not in this library).

summary.zh: Schonell 法官梳理默示承诺法理：其目的在保护隐私、维护保密、鼓励充分坦诚的披露；约束范围及于所有澳洲法院/仲裁、当事人及其律师与收到文件的第三方（含专家），且不限于文件、亦涵盖信息；只有法院可解除（Holpitt）。
summary.en: Schonell J reviewed the law: the undertaking protects privacy, preserves confidentiality and encourages full and frank disclosure; it binds all Australian courts/arbitration, parties and their lawyers, and third parties (including experts) who receive documents, and covers information as well as documents; only the court can release it (Holpitt).

prop.zh: 默示承诺的目的、约束范围与解除主体。
prop.en: Purpose, reach and who may release the undertaking.

src.zh: Kuang 援引并赞同 Chakora 的法理梳理，本案再予引用。
src.en: Kuang adopted Chakora's analysis with approval, and the case relies on it.

note.zh: ✦ 同庭先例（未纳入本资料库）。

court.zh: FCFCOA 第 1 庭（全席上诉）· 2023

summary.zh: 确认：文件在另案是否可采，与是否解除默示承诺无关——可采性是另一法院的事。
summary.en: Confirmed that whether a document is admissible in the other proceedings is irrelevant to whether to release the undertaking — admissibility is a matter for the other court.

prop.zh: 可采性属另一法院，非解除承诺的考量。
prop.en: Admissibility is for the other court, not a factor on release.

src.zh: 本案据 Littlefield 区分「用途」与「可采性」。
src.en: The case used Littlefield to separate 'use' from 'admissibility'.

note.zh: ✦ 全席上诉判例（未纳入本资料库）。
note.en: ✦ Full Court authority (not in this library).

t.zh: Holpitt Pty Ltd v Varimu Pty Ltd
t.en: Holpitt Pty Ltd v Varimu Pty Ltd

summary.zh: 默示承诺是对法院作出的，因此只有法院能解除一方的承诺。
summary.en: Because the undertaking is given to the court, only the court may release a party from it.

prop.zh: 唯有法院可解除默示承诺。
prop.en: Only the court may release the undertaking.

src.zh: 经 Chakora 引用，本案再予援引。
src.en: Cited via Chakora and relied on here.

note.zh: ✦ 联邦法院判例（未纳入本资料库）。
note.en: ✦ Federal Court authority (not in this library).

fulltext:
REASONS FOR JUDGMENT

INTRODUCTION

The primary proceedings before the Court relate to the parties’ competing applications for property adjustment. Those proceedings were commenced by the wife, Ms Florou, upon the filing of her Initiating Application on 19 December 2025.
In that application, the wife seeks alteration of the parties’ property interests and provision for adult child maintenance, as well as a raft of interim orders including orders for spousal maintenance. The husband, Mr Florou, also seeks adjustment of the parties’ property in his Response to Initiating Application filed 9 February 2026.
In early 2026, the wife caused a Writ and Statement of Claim to be filed in the Common Law Division of the Supreme Court of Victoria. In those proceedings, the wife, who is the plaintiff, seeks damages, including exemplary damages, from the husband, alleging that in a Statutory Declaration provided by him to third parties, he defamed her, breached her confidence and disclosed and misused private information and thereby intruded upon her privacy (“the Supreme Court proceedings”).
In early 2026, the husband caused a summons to issue in the Supreme Court proceedings seeking a transfer of those proceedings (“the Transfer Application”) for determination by this Court pursuant to the Jurisdiction of Courts (Cross-Vesting) Act 1987 (Vic). It is the husband’s contention that the Supreme Court proceedings and the parties’ competing property applications in this Court are a single justiciable controversy capable of determination by this Court.
The husband’s summons filed in the Supreme Court proceedings is listed for hearing in mid-2026. The final hearing of the Supreme Court proceedings is listed as a five to seven-day matter to commence in late 2026.[1] The property proceedings before this Court are in their early stages and any trial, if necessary, will not be listed until 2027.
By Application in a Proceeding filed 10 April 2026, the husband seeks to be released from his implied undertaking to the Court, in accordance with the principle established in Harman v Secretary of State for the Home Department [1983] 1 AC 280 (“Harman”), and affirmed in this country by the High Court of Australia in Hearne v Street [2008] HCA 36; (2008) 235 CLR 125, as well as the obligations imposed by s 114Q of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) (“the Act”) and r. 6.04 of the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Family Law) Rules 2021 (“the Rules”) in relation to two affidavits filed in these proceedings, being his affidavit filed 9 February 2026 and the affidavit of the wife filed 19 December 2025.
The basis for that application is set out in the affidavit filed on behalf of the husband on 10 April 2026 where he deposes at [9] that:-
...To enable me to provide instructions to my lawyers in support of the Transfer Application, I understand they will need to rely on the material filed by [the wife] and me in these proceedings. I also understand that I have certain obligations to keep documents in these proceedings confidential, and therefore, I seek the Court’s permission to make the documents...available to use in support of the Transfer Application.
The wife opposes the husband’s application and seeks that it be dismissed. Further, she seeks orders that the husband pay her costs of and incidental to the application.
For the reasons that follow, I am satisfied that the husband’s Application in a Proceeding ought be dismissed. In circumstances where there were no submissions made on behalf of either party at the hearing with respect to the wife’s claim for costs, I will make orders for the filing of written submissions with respect to those matters and subject to objection, will determine that application on the papers, in Chambers.

DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

The husband relied upon the following documents:-
(a) Application in a Proceeding filed 10 April 2026;

(b) Affidavit of the husband filed 10 April 2026;

(c) Outline of Submissions filed 1 May 2026;

(d) Exhibit H-1, being the Writ and Statement of Claim filed by the wife in the Supreme Court proceedings; and

(e) Exhibit H-2, being the Defence filed on behalf of the husband in the Supreme Court proceedings.

The wife relied upon the following documents:-
(a) Response to Application in a Proceeding filed 24 April 2026;

(b) Affidavit of the wife filed 24 April 2026;

(c) Outline of Case document filed 4 May 2026;

(d) Amended Initiating Application filed 10 March 2026;

(e) Financial Statement filed 19 December 2025; and

(f) Affidavit of the wife filed 19 December 2025.

LEGAL PRINCIPLES

Rule 6.04 of the Rules governs the use of documents produced in proceedings before this Court. Rule 6.04(1) provides as follows:-
(1) Unless the court gives permission, a person who inspects, copies or receives a document, in relation to a proceeding, under these Rules or an order:

(a) must use the document for the purpose of the proceeding only; and

(b) must not otherwise disclose the contents of the document, or give a copy of it, to any other person.

That rule codifies the obligations imposed by the implied undertaking that parties have to Courts, as confirmed by the High Court of Australia in Hearne v Street [2008] HCA 36; (2008) 235 CLR 125 at [154] as follows:-

Where one party to litigation is compelled, either by reason of a rule of court, or by reasons of a specific order of the court, or otherwise, to disclose documents or information, the party obtaining the disclosure cannot, without the leave of the court, use it for any purpose other than that for which it was given unless it is received into evidence.

The use of documents or information obtained in proceedings is otherwise governed by r 15.26 of the Rules which provides as follows:-
(1) The court may grant permission for a person to use a document or information obtained in proceedings for another purpose, including in proceedings before another court or tribunal, if the person can satisfy the court that special circumstances exist to permit the person to use the document or information for that other purpose.
(2) In this rule:
document includes, but is not limited to, the following:
(a) a document filed in a proceeding in accordance with these Rules or an order;

(b) a document produced in accordance with a disclosure obligation or a subpoena;

(c) a document admitted into evidence in a proceeding;
(d) a document provided to the court in accordance with section 67ZBD or 67ZBE of the Family Law Act;
(e) a family report or single expert report;

(f) reasons for judgment whether anonymised or not.
Note 1: For restrictions on the use of a document produced in compliance with the duty of disclosure, see rule 6.04.

Note 2: For restrictions on the use of a document produced in compliance with a subpoena for production, see rule 6.36.

Note 3: Nothing in this rule limits the ability of a person to use a document for another purpose if the use is authorised by other legislation, for example, the Assessment Act.
That rule confirms that in order for the Court to grant permission for a person to use a document obtained in the proceedings for another purpose, including proceedings in another court, that person must satisfy the court that special circumstances exist to permit such use of the document or information.
The onus is on the husband to establish that there is some special circumstance which takes this matter beyond the ordinary course such that he ought be relieved from the Harman obligation.
In the decision of Kuang & Kuang [2025] FedCFamC1A 31 (“Kuang”), the Full Court considered the question of “special circumstances” and helpfully summarised previously decided cases. In particular, it confirmed with approval the analysis of the jurisprudence of the implied undertaking provided by Schonell J in Chakora & Bhander [2023] FedCFamC1F 127 at [8]–[10], [13]–[14], where His Honour stated:-
The purpose of the implied undertaking has variously been described as protecting privacy, preserving confidentiality and to encourage full and frank disclosure in the knowledge that such production will be protected.
The obligation extends to all Australian courts and tribunals as well as arbitration proceedings. It binds not just parties but their solicitors and counsel and third parties including experts who are in receipt of documents generated or produced by the litigation. It is not just limited to documents but covers information.
As the implied undertaking arises in the context of litigation and is given to the Court, then it is only the Court that may release a party from the undertaking (Holpitt Pty Ltd v Varimu Pty Ltd (1991) 29 FCR 576).
...
In Springfield Nominees Pty Ltd v Bridgelands Securities Ltd (1992) 38 FCR 217 (“Springfield Nominees”), Wilcox J said at 225:

... For “special circumstances” to exist it is enough that there is a special feature of the case which affords a reason for modifying or releasing the undertaking and is not usually present. The matter then becomes one of the proper exercise of the court's discretion, many factors being relevant. It is neither possible nor desirable to propound an exhaustive list of those factors. But plainly they include the nature of the document, the circumstances under which it came into existence, the attitude of the author of the document and any prejudice the author may sustain, whether the document pre-existed litigation or was created for that purpose and therefore expected to enter the public domain, the nature of the information in the document (in particular whether it contains personal data or commercially sensitive information), the circumstances in which the document came into the hands of the applicant for leave and, perhaps most important of all, the likely contribution of the document to achieving justice in the second proceeding.
His Honour did not import words of limitation to the discretion. It is clear that the discretion is appropriately wide to fit the circumstances of each particular scenario.
The question of whether a document is admissible in the other proceedings is not relevant to the determination of whether a release from the Harman obligation ought be granted; the admissibility or otherwise of such material is a matter for the other court (Littlefield & Pemble [2023] FedCFamC1A 198; (2018) 67 Fam LR 603 at [37]). However, the Full Court in Kuang confirmed that whilst the admissibility of the material is not a relevant consideration, its purpose or possible use in the other proceedings is a matter which may be taken into account, stating at [49] as follows:-
...In considering whether or not release from the implied undertaking should be granted, the court from which the release is sought can, and will often need to, have regard, more broadly, to the purpose or possible use of that document in the other court; in other words, why the release of the document is sought. Without considering for what purpose an applicant wants to use a document, or how it might be used, it may be difficult to determine whether there is a good reason to release the document from the implied undertaking. However, that is qualitatively different from the first court determining whether the document could actually be used, and be admissible in, the other court.

CONSIDERATION

It was submitted on behalf of the husband that the special circumstances which support his release from the implied obligation with respect to the two affidavits are:-
(a) The commonality of issues as between the two proceedings. It was submitted that the wife’s alleged mental health issues were relevant to both sets of proceedings and the evidence adduced with respect to those matters in this Court was relevant and necessary in the Supreme Court proceedings; and

(b) That, were the documents not available for use in the Supreme Court proceedings, the husband would be deprived of the opportunity to properly proceed with his application for transfer of those proceedings to this Court.

It was further submitted on behalf of the husband that an assessment of the relevance of the material to the Transfer Application is a matter for the Supreme Court; it is not necessary for this Court to determine the relevance of the material to that proceeding.
As noted in Kuang (supra), in determining whether a party ought be released from the Harman obligation, a Court may have regard to the purpose or possible use of the material in the other proceedings. Accordingly, I am satisfied that I may consider the connection between the documents sought to be released and the Supreme Court proceedings, and the purpose or possible use of the documents in those proceedings. Absent that consideration, I may not be in a position to assess relevant factors, such as the potential contribution of those documents to achieving justice in the Supreme Court proceedings.
The background to the Supreme Court proceedings is as follows. At the relevant time the husband was president of a professional association. In early 2025, a sexually explicit image of the husband was posted to his social media account. An executive from another business that the husband works with was tagged in the image. The husband denied that he posted that image to his social media account.
Following the publication of that image, two investigations were conducted in relation to the post on the husband’s social media account. In response to those two investigations, the husband allegedly published a Statutory Declaration disclosing matters personal to the wife; the wife contends that the husband attributed responsibility for the posting of the explicit photo on his social media account to the wife. The wife contends that she was defamed, that her confidence was breached, and that her privacy was invaded by the husband as a result of his disclosure of matters in that Statutory Declaration, the content of which she alleges was widely disseminated.
The wife alleges that the confidential information contained in that Statutory Declaration included information about the wife’s:-
Sexual history;
Mental health;
Medical history;
Private and personal relationships; and
Engagement with law enforcement.[2]
The central issue for consideration in the Supreme Court proceedings is whether, by disseminating the information contained in his Statutory Declaration, the husband defamed the wife, breached her confidence, or invaded her privacy.
The husband contends that he ought be permitted to disclose the affidavit of the wife filed 19 December 2025 for the purposes of the Transfer Application in the Supreme Court proceedings. In particular, senior counsel for the husband submitted that paragraph 64 of the wife’s affidavit was relevant to that application.
Paragraph 64 of the wife’s affidavit details the wife’s allegations of family violence and risk. She there deposes to alleged conduct by the husband which she asserts evidences his coercive control and family violence perpetrated during the relationship and in the post-separation period. The allegations include occasions where it is said that the husband verbally abused the wife, that he denigrated her, including in the presence of the parties’ children, that he yelled and swore at her, sent her abusive texts, that he has exerted financial control over her and that he has undermined her and misled others as to the state of her mental health.
That affidavit was sworn by the wife in support of her interim application for urgent financial relief including part-property settlement and was filed prior to the commencement of the Supreme Court proceedings. It was submitted on behalf of the wife, and I accept, that that affidavit was prepared for the sole purpose of the family law proceedings and at the time it was sworn, the wife’s expectation was that the affidavit would remain wholly confidential and not be used for any other purpose.
By the time the husband filed his affidavit in response on 9 February 2026 the Supreme Court proceedings had commenced. In that affidavit the husband denies the wife’s allegations as to family violence allegedly perpetrated by him. He also deposes as to his alleged concerns for the wife’s mental health, the wife’s alleged suicide attempts between 2020 and 2024 and her alleged use of illicit substances.[3] Having regard to the interlocutory issues then before this Court, that evidence was of seemingly little relevance to those proceedings.
It was submitted on behalf of the wife that the Supreme Court proceedings relate to a discrete event, being the asserted act of defamation by the husband. Further, it was submitted that the commonality between the two proceedings ends with the names of the parties. Having regard to the nature of the proceedings in each Court, there is much force in that submission.
The property proceedings in this Court involve the application of s 79 of the Act; that inquiry will necessitate the identification and valuation of the parties’ interests, an assessment of their respective contributions during the relationship and a consideration of matters relating to their current and future circumstances. In contrast, the Supreme Court proceedings involve considerations as to whether the dissemination by the husband of his Statutory Declaration to third parties has defamed the wife within the meaning of the Defamation Act 2005 (Vic), breached her confidence, or invaded her privacy.
Having regard to the nature of the applications in the two Courts and the relief sought by the parties in each, I am not satisfied that there is a commonality of issues, as submitted on behalf of the husband. That view is bolstered having regard to the concession made on behalf of the wife with respect to her property application, namely that she will not be seeking an adjustment as a result of family violence allegedly perpetrated by the husband. Given that concession, the wife will not be adducing evidence or require findings in relation to those matters at trial. Further, in light of that concession, whilst the wife’s health is a matter that may be relevant to consideration of her contributions during the marriage or her future circumstances, any evidence about her health will likely be confined to those limited issues.
The affidavits filed by the parties in these proceedings traverse a range of deeply sensitive and personal information related not only to the husband and the wife, but also their children. For example, the wife deposes as to:-
Her diagnosis with complex post-traumatic stress disorder arising from childhood sexual abuse;
The circumstances of the parties’ adult child who has a genetic condition and an intellectual disability;
The financial support provided to the parties’ child under the NDIS;
The financial support provided by the husband to the wife and the parties’ adult child; and
The parties’ property, income and investments.
There does not appear to be a nexus between the evidence contained in those affidavits and the issues raised in the Supreme Court proceedings. Were the husband released from his Harman obligations, not only would information personal to the parties be released but also information personal to their adult children. As those proceedings are being conducted in an open court there is a significant risk that the contents of those affidavits may be released to the public. In the husband’s Outline of Submissions filed 1 May 2026 the husband seeks to attribute responsibility for the interest of those proceedings to the wife.[4] However, there is no admissible evidence before the Court with respect to those assertions.
The husband also relies upon the fact that he has sought orders in the Supreme Court proceedings that the documents released by this Court remain confidential and not be available for inspection on the Court file. However, that the husband seeks such order does not ensure that it will be made; the risk of release of the parties’ and their children’s confidential information remains.
It was submitted by counsel for the husband that his application for transfer of proceedings to this Court would be rendered nugatory and the proper administration of justice thwarted if the husband was not released from the Harman obligation with respect to the two affidavits. However, beyond those assertions, that submission was not advanced or developed.
In circumstances where the issues confronting the Supreme Court proceedings involve separate and discrete issues to those raised pursuant to s 79 of the Act in this Court (noting the concession made on behalf of the wife with respect to the family violence allegations), I am not satisfied that there is a nexus or “commonality of issues” between the two proceedings as contended by the husband.
Accordingly, I am not satisfied that the husband has established that a special circumstance exists to permit his release from the Harman obligation with respect to the parties’ affidavits. Further, even if such special circumstance were established, I am not satisfied that the discretion to release him from that undertaking ought be exercised, particularly having regard to:-
The sensitive and highly personal matters contained pertaining to the parties and their adult children in the affidavits sought to be released;
The fact that those affidavits were sworn and filed for the purposes of these proceedings and as such were afforded the protection of confidentiality pursuant to s 114Q of the Act;
Other than the parties in both proceedings being the same, there is little commonality in the issues confronting each Court; and
The paucity of evidence adduced by the husband as to how he would be disadvantaged were he prevented from using the affidavits in the Supreme Court proceedings. No submission was made on behalf of the husband that illuminated how the documents sought to be released were relevant to the issues raised in the Supreme Court proceedings or how those documents could assist in the proper determination of that case or the administration of justice.
Having regard to those matters, the husband’s Application in a Proceeding will be dismissed. The wife seeks that the husband pay her costs of and incidental to that application. Neither the husband nor the wife addressed the issue of costs during the hearing. Accordingly, I will make orders for the filing of submissions with respect to the wife’s application for costs, and subject to any submission to the contrary, will determine that application on the papers in Chambers.
